The growth rate in the price of shares from the highest value during the quarter ended December 31, 2010 to the closing price on February 11, 2011 can be calculated using the following formula:

Growth Rate = (Closing Price - Highest Price) / Highest Price \* 100

Using the values from the table, the highest price during the quarter ending December 31, 2010 was $53.14 and the closing price on February 11, 2011 was $56.73. Plugging these values into the formula, we get:

Growth Rate = ($56.73 - $53.14) / $53.14 \* 100 = 7.89%

Therefore, the growth rate in the price of shares from the highest value during the quarter ended December 31, 2010 to the closing price on February 11, 2011 is 7.89%.
